title: Individual and city-level variations in heat-related road traffic deaths in Latin America

meta_data.Data_Name: SALURBAL daily road traffic mortality microdata harmonized from national statistical agencies
meta_data.Data_summary: Daily counts of road traffic deaths constructed from individual death records coded with ICD-10 external cause codes, including subcategories for pedestrian, cyclist, motorcyclist and motor vehicle occupant deaths. The study uses these daily city time series as the primary outcome, applying redistribution for ill-defined external-cause codes and creating multiple imputations to account for misclassification and incomplete coding detail.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: Country-level mortality data were obtained from national statistical agencies with some publicly available and others available via Drexel University upon request, Location: Data availability, Confidence: high
meta_data.Geographic_Coverage: Latin America > Brazil, Mexico, Chile, El Salvador, Panama, Costa Rica > 272 cities

meta_data.Data_summary: A harmonized multicountry urban health data platform that consolidates environmental, social and health-related data across Latin American cities. In this paper it serves as the integration framework for linking mortality, temperature exposure and city-level contextual measures across the analyzed cities.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: Access to restricted data requires a data use agreement and approval of a study proposal by the SALURBAL proposal and publications committee, Location: Data availability, Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_summary: Hourly near-surface air temperature reanalysis at gridded resolution, aggregated to daily mean temperature and then population-weighted for each city. These daily city temperatures are the exposure variable, converted to city-specific temperature percentiles for pooled modeling, with missing grid cells addressed via imputation using coarser ERA5 and terrain variables.
meta_data.Category: Satellite remote sensing imagery (optical, SAR, night lights)
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: The exposure variable was the daily mean temperature derived from hourly temperature data from the fifth generation European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts atmospheric reanalysis ERA5-Land, Location: Methods Outcome exposure and effect modifiers, Confidence: high
meta_data.Geographic_Coverage: Latin America > Brazil, Mexico, Chile, El Salvador, Panama, Costa Rica > 272 cities

meta_data.Data_Name: ERA5 hourly data on single levels (reanalysis era5 single levels) used for temperature-gap imputation
meta_data.Data_summary: Gridded hourly meteorological reanalysis data resampled to coarser spatial resolution and used as auxiliary predictors to impute missing ERA5-Land temperature grid cells within city boundaries, before population-weighting and daily aggregation.
meta_data.Category: Satellite remote sensing imagery (optical, SAR, night lights)
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: These missing temperature values were imputed using a random forest regression model integrating resampled ERA5 temperature data at a 31-km resolution along with elevation and aspect, Location: Methods Outcome exposure and effect modifiers, Confidence: high
meta_data.Geographic_Coverage: Latin America > Brazil, Mexico, Chile, El Salvador, Panama, Costa Rica > 272 cities

meta_data.Data_summary: Gridded population counts used to population-weight gridded temperature data to the city level and to provide annual population denominators for calculating city-level road traffic mortality rates.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: Publicly available datasets used in this study include ERA5-Land temperature WorldPop population and Global Urban Footprint urban form, Location: Data availability, Confidence: high
meta_data.Geographic_Coverage: Latin America > Brazil, Mexico, Chile, El Salvador, Panama, Costa Rica > 272 cities

meta_data.Data_Name: Global Urban Footprint (GUF) urban form dataset
meta_data.Data_summary: A remote-sensing-derived global map of built-up or urban form used within the SALURBAL workflow to characterize urban areas and support city-level spatial delineations and built environment characterization referenced in the study.
meta_data.Category: Building footprints and land use maps
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: Publicly available datasets used in this study include ERA5-Land temperature WorldPop population and Global Urban Footprint urban form, Location: Data availability, Confidence: medium

meta_data.Data_summary: Crowdsourced street network geometry used to define intersections as nodes and links as street segments, enabling computation of mean street segment length as a city-level effect modifier representing street-network block/segment structure.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: In the street network data sourced from OpenStreetMap each intersection was considered as a node and the link connecting each node as a street segment, Location: Methods Outcome exposure and effect modifiers, Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_Name: Google Maps Distance Matrix API travel time estimates
meta_data.Data_summary: Estimated peak-hour travel times generated from the Google Maps Distance Matrix API for randomly sampled origin-destination pairs within each city street network at multiple peak-hour time points. These data are summarized to a city-level mean peak-hour travel time effect modifier representing commute duration.
meta_data.Category: Human mobility traces (GPS, transit card, ride-hailing)
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: The metric was derived from the Google Maps Distance Matrix application programming interface using estimated delays for 30 randomly selected origin-destination pairs at seven different time points, Location: Methods Outcome exposure and effect modifiers, Confidence: high
meta_data.Geographic_Coverage: Latin America > Brazil, Mexico, Chile, El Salvador, Panama, Costa Rica > 272 cities

meta_data.Data_Name: Paper-level data deposit from Data availability GitHub portal
meta_data.Data_summary: A project GitHub portal referenced as the access point for multiple publicly available datasets used in the study, including temperature, population and urban form inputs.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: Publicly available datasets used in this study include ERA5-Land temperature WorldPop population and Global Urban Footprint urban form available via GitHub at https://github.com/Drexel-UHC/salurbal_heat, Location: Data availability, Confidence: high
